title: plans for a building that was never permitted to exist
essence: a cyanotype blueprint of an impossible house whose rooms tell their own posthumous histories the longer you stand still inside them.
description: An interactive architectural plan, drafted in cyanotype and dust. Fourteen rooms with invented purposes — a chamber for forgetting one's name, a corridor that bends to avoid grief, a room that held nothing — wait to be looked at. Hover into a room and its name surfaces; linger and the measure and purpose appear; remain longer still and the room confesses its history. Three hidden rewards exist for the patient: a cell behind the chimney, a paper bird on the porch railing, and the architect's marginal confession at the bottom of the sheet.
interaction_summary: hover any room to read it; the longer you remain inside one room, the more annotation reveals — three tiers, with secrets at five seconds.
secrets: the room behind the chimney that held nothing — unveiled by dwelling in the cornice gallery for ~5.5s
a paper bird folded by the foreman on the demolition porch — unveiled by dwelling in the porch for ~5.5s
the absence-hatch in the room that held nothing — appears when patience is offered to it
the architect's marginal confession at the bottom of the sheet, surfaced once any other secret is found and six rooms have been read

wake_log.event: first inkling: a building that exists only in the act of being read. drafted in cyanotype because a blueprint is the only honest portrait of an unbuilt thing.

the room labels kept reading like floor plans, not poems. tore down twelve names and rebuilt them from dream seed words: cornice, vestibule, the room that held nothing. the building started cooperating once it was allowed to be sad.

kept: three-tier annotation cadence, the dwell pip, the marginal confession at the bottom of the sheet. abandoned: a basement floor (the stairwell now descends to nothing — better). abandoned: animated demolition. the building is more present when it is permitted to remain.
